In *al-Tahdhib*, with its chain from Safwan ibn Mihran, who said: I said to Abu ‘Abdillah (عليه‌السلام): "A Muslim woman comes to me, having known me by my work, and I know her by her Islam; she has no mahram (محرم) — *a male relative whom she is forbidden to marry*, so I carry her [on my mount]." He said: "Then carry her, for the believer is a mahram to the female believer." Then he recited this verse: **وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَالْمُؤْمِناتُ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِياءُ بَعْضٍ** "The faithful, men and women, are allies of one another..." [9:71].

**I say:** Al-‘Ayyashi narrated it in his *Tafsir* from Safwan al-Jammal from him (عليه‌السلام).

In the *Tafsir* of al-‘Ayyashi, from Thuwayr from ‘Ali ibn al-Husayn (عليه‌السلام), who said: "When the people of Paradise are in Paradise, and the friend (ولي) of Allah enters his gardens and dwellings and reclines, every believer on his couch, his servants surrounding him, the fruits hanging low over him, the springs gushing around him, the rivers flowing beneath him, the carpets spread for him, the cushions set for him, and the servants bring him whatever his soul desires before he asks them for it." He said: "And the houris (الحور العين) — *the pure, dark-eyed maidens of Paradise* — come out to him from the gardens, and they remain in that state for as long as Allah wills."

"Then the All-Compelling (الجبار) looks upon them and says to them: 'O My friends, people of My obedience, and inhabitants of My Paradise in My proximity! Shall I inform you of something better than what you are in?' They will say: 'Our Lord, and what thing is better than what we are in—amidst what our souls desire and our eyes delight in of blessings in the proximity of the Generous?'"

He said: "Then He repeats the statement to them, and they say: 'Our Lord, yes! Then bring us something better than what we are in.' Then He, Blessed and Exalted is He, says to them: 'My pleasure (رضاي) with you and My love for you is better and greater than what you are in.'" He said: "Then they say: 'Yes, O our Lord! Your pleasure with us and Your love for us is better and more pleasant for our souls.'"

Then ‘Ali ibn al-Husayn (عليه‌السلام) recited this verse: **وَعَدَ اللهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِناتِ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها وَمَساكِنَ طَيِّبَةً فِي جَنَّاتِ عَدْنٍ وَرِضْوانٌ مِنَ اللهِ أَكْبَرُ ذلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ** "Allah has promised the faithful, men and women, gardens with conduits running amidst them, to abide in them [forever], and goodly dwellings in the Gardens of Eden. And Allah’s pleasure is greater [than all these]; that is the great success." [9:72].

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, Ibn Mardawayh narrated from Jabir who said: The Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) said: "When the people of Paradise enter Paradise, Allah says: 'Do you desire anything that I may increase for you?' They say: 'O our Lord, does anything remain that You have not granted us?' He says: 'Yes, My pleasure (رضائي), so I shall never be angry with you ever again.'"

**I say:** This meaning is reported in many narrations from the paths of both sects.

In *Jami‘ al-Jawami‘*, from Abu al-Darda’ from the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله): "Eden (عدن) is the abode of Allah which no eye has seen and which has not occurred to the heart of any mortal. None shall dwell in it except three: the prophets, the truthful (الصديقون), and the martyrs (الشهداء). Allah says: 'Blessed is he who enters you.'"

**I say:** The specificity of the inhabitants of Paradise in the narration does not contradict their generality in the verse, due to the indication of His saying, the Exalted: **وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِاللهِ وَرُسُلِهِ أُولئِكَ هُمُ الصِّدِّيقُونَ وَالشُّهَداءُ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ** "Those who have faith in Allah and His apostles—it is they who are the truthful and the witnesses with their Lord..." [57:19], showing that Allah the Glorified will join the generality of the believers with the truthful and the martyrs.

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi, regarding His saying, the Exalted: **يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ جاهِدِ الْكُفَّارَ وَالْمُنافِقِينَ** "O Prophet! Wage jihad against the faithless and the hypocrites..." [9:73]:

He said: My father narrated to me from Abu ‘Umayr from Abu Basir from Abu Ja‘far (عليه‌السلام) who said: "Wage jihad against the disbelievers and the hypocrites by enjoining the obligatory duties (الفرائض)."

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, al-Bayhaqi narrated in *Shu‘ab al-Iman* from Ibn Mas‘ud who said: When **يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ جاهِدِ الْكُفَّارَ وَالْمُنافِقِينَ** was revealed, the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) was commanded to wage jihad with his hand; if he could not, then with his heart; if he could not, then with his tongue; and if he could not, then let him meet him with a frowning face.

**I say:** There is confusion in the narration regarding the order of its parts, as jihad with the heart comes after all else, yet it was placed in between.
